title: TxQueueEntry.enqueue_time_ms uses monotonic clock but TxQueue may use different clock
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/tx_queue.py:83, 140

TxQueueEntry uses time.monotonic() for enqueue_time_ms:

    enqueue_time_ms: int = field(default_factory=lambda: int(time.monotonic() * 1000))

But TxQueue can be initialized with a custom clock:

    self._clock = clock or (lambda: int(time.monotonic() * 1000))

If a test passes a custom clock to TxQueue, the TxQueueEntry still uses the real monotonic clock for enqueue_time_ms. This causes incorrect latency calculations when the clocks disagree.

Fix: Have TxQueue pass its clock to TxQueueEntry creation, or compute enqueue_time inside push() using the TxQueue's clock (which is what currently happens at line 211-212).
close_reason: Fixed TxQueueEntry to require clock-consistent enqueue_time_ms; refactored ensure_can_push to prevent mutation during check and fixed missing return in push preempt path for reservation consistency. All tests, ruff, and mypy now pass. Commit: 277ad575a
